title: The Changing Room
description: A vivid, fast-paced portrait of teenagers on the brink, set in and around a swimming pool, The Changing Room follows a group of young people as they navigate identity, friendship, and the restless energy of growing up. Moments collide and overlap – worries are shared, friendships formed as the ground begins to shift beneath their feet. This is a play about losing your footing and stepping into who you might become... The award-winning Kaleidoscope Theatre Company brings this vibrant, funny, and touching play to life with its trademark ensemble work and bold staging, offering an honest look at the exhilaration and uncertainty of youth – and one magnificently embarrassing dad.

reviews.reviewer: Lela Tredwell Late-night talk radio typically provides a place on the air waves
reviews.excerpt: Late-night talk radio typically provides a place on the air waves where softly-spoken conversations, lonely confessions, and mundane musings act as an antidote to the dark hours. In their new improvised show, Bird and Tax Collector aim to provide one such refuge for the insomniacs, the night owls and the shift workers.
